summary: CocoIndex child tombstones are short-lived reconciliation markers. For flat SQLite causal_edges, keep active edges hard-deleted and add a separate tombstone/audit table plus unified deletion helper and orphan sweep.

claim: CocoIndex tombstones are reconciliation markers created when stored child existence diverges from current children, then consumed by a child-component GC/delete path.
evidence: rust/core/src/engine/execution.rs:612-620 scans stored ChildExistence rows by prefix
rust/core/src/engine/execution.rs:631-646 deletes stored children missing from current state
rust/core/src/engine/execution.rs:725-742 scans tombstones and dispatches component.delete
rust/core/src/engine/execution.rs:1472-1498 removes the tombstone after successful child processing

claim: CocoIndex already identifies generation as the missing race-protection primitive for child lifecycle deletes and reinserts.
evidence: rust/core/src/state/db_schema.rs:311-319 ChildExistenceInfo stores node_type and includes a TODO to add generation for asynchronous deletion races

claim: Our causal_edges table has no lifecycle state, tombstone table, persisted generation, or foreign-key cascade; active queries assume every row is live.
evidence: lib/search/vector-index-schema.ts:607-622 defines causal_edges columns without deleted_at or tombstone fields
lib/storage/causal-edges.ts:525-559 reads outgoing/incoming rows directly from causal_edges
lib/storage/causal-edges.ts:781-791 computes stats over all causal_edges

claim: Explicit memory_delete and memory_bulk_delete paths already hard-delete causal edges transactionally, but stale indexed-record deletion can bypass causal edge cleanup and leave orphans until repair.
evidence: handlers/memory-crud-delete.ts:94-118 calls causalEdges.deleteEdgesForMemory in the single-delete transaction
handlers/memory-bulk-delete.ts:221-255 calls deleteEdgesForMemory and propagates failures
handlers/memory-index.ts:310-325 deletes stale records via vectorIndex.deleteMemory
lib/search/vector-index-mutations.ts:103-110 ancillary delete list does not include causal_edges

claim: Separate causal_edge_tombstones fits better than soft-deleting causal_edges because it preserves audit and sweep metadata without forcing traversal, stats, and relation-coverage filters into every active query.
evidence: lib/storage/causal-edges.ts:598-655 traverses directly over getEdgesFrom/getEdgesTo results
handlers/causal-graph.ts:799-810 stats and coverage query active causal_edges directly
handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:555-567 already offers an autoRepair hook for orphan edge cleanup

claim: Path rename with stable memory id does not require edge movement because causal_edges stores memory ids; delete-plus-recreate reindex should be treated as endpoint deletion unless a future alias/rekey layer explicitly remaps identity.
evidence: handlers/memory-crud-update.ts:186-191 reads updated file_path and canonical_file_path while retaining the same id
lib/search/vector-index-schema.ts:607-610 stores source_id and target_id rather than file paths
